sessions.lever: register-alloc-pure-c levers (store reorder, new_var-early, narrow/wide type, +4-first); none flipped var_v0 off $v1

reviewer.evidence: The only forms that touched the floor (11) carry two empty `do { } while(0)` scheduler barriers (test #1: no semantic purpose). Clean-C form with do-while removed scored 16 (worse). No clean candidate below HEAD's 12 was found, so candidate.c is NOT saved; the cheat-carrying best form is preserved under rejected/plus4_first_dowhile.c.

remaining_gap.summary: Coupled register-rotation: target carries var_v0 (0x46/0x32) in $v0 and the 0xFC constant in $v1 (preset into the inner-if beqz delay slot, re-materialized for +0x14). GCC's global allocator glues var_v0 to $v1 (pseudo 74 -> reg 3 in the .greg) because it is assigned right after the `andi $2` condition test while $v0 is reused by the inner byte-store constants. Plus a 10-store reorder in the arg0>=4 ELSE block.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 6 `subst` (rename $2<->$3 + store-offset rewrites), 1 `delete` (redundant 0xFC reload), 1 `insert_after` (lost-codegen var_v0=0x46), 1 `reorder` (ELSE 10-store permutation). All 9 are the register-swap + store-order the clean-C levers could not reproduce.

next_hypotheses: Instrumented cc1 (BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG / register-alloc-deep-dive recipe) to read the allocno priority that pins var_v0 to $v1 — the .greg confirms pseudo 74 in reg 3 but not WHY it outranks $v0 for var_v0.
Try making the inner byte-store constants (0xC3/0x1E/0xC8) share ONE local so they stop monopolizing $v0, freeing $v0 for var_v0 (Lever A live-range shrink on the OTHER pseudo).

Cross-ref the matched simpler family (func_8006133C et al.) — those were single-store; this is the same $v0/$v1 tie but coupled across a branch merge + ELSE reorder, likely the register-alloc-pure-c 'confirmed limits' class.
If clean levers stay measured-negative, this is a candidate for canonical-asm review (user decision), like marionation_Exec's register-rotation wall.

rejected_forms.form: Drop do-while barriers + new_var, write 0xFC/var_v0 literals, reorder THEN+ELSE stores to target order
rejected_forms.reason: Removing the barriers drops insn count to 77 (target 79) and diverges structurally; clean-C is FARTHER, not closer.

rejected_forms.form: new_var = 0xFC assigned BEFORE the inner if (to occupy $v1 early)
rejected_forms.reason: GCC re-materializes 0xFC anyway; var_v0 stays in $v1. Worse, not a cheat per se but measured-negative.

rejected_forms.form: +4-before-0x14 store order + two do { } while(0) barriers
rejected_forms.reason: Best measured, but carries empty do-while scheduler barriers (cheat-reviewer test #1 FAIL). Preserved under rejected/plus4_first_dowhile.c.
